title: herniaria glabra subsp. rotundifolia
intro: Herniaria glabra subsp. rotundifolia, also known as round-leaved rampion, is a flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as a diuretic. Herniaria glabra subsp. rotundifolia is typically used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
